perturbation.original_text: Recipient will notify each emp loyee, agent, affiliate , representative, or consultant involved in the business discussions or who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement and shall require such person to enter i nto an agreement with terms similar to this Agreement .
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may notify each employee, agent, affiliate, representative, or consultant who may have any occasion to view, handle, or obtain any of the Confidential Information, of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'will notify' and 'shall require' to 'may notify', thereby removing the mandatory requirement for those accessing confidential information to be informed of the agreement's terms and to sign a similar agreement. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement aims to ensure all individuals handling confidential information are bound by its terms, but this modification makes it optional.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient acknowledge s and agree s that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Disclosing Party would be irreparably and immediately harmed and could not be made whole by monetary damages. It is accordingly agreed that Disclosing Party, in addition to any other remed y to which it may be entitled in law or equity, shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement, and to compel specific performance of this Agreement, without the need for proof of actual damages.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that the recipient notify and bind all individuals handling confidential information to the agreement's terms. The changed text makes this notification and agreement optional. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that any breach of the agreement will cause irreparable harm and entitles the disclosing party to injunctive relief and specific performance. If individuals handling confidential information are not bound by the agreement due to the changed text's optional notification, the disclosing party's ability to prevent breaches and compel specific performance is compromised, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall protect the disclosed Confidential Information by using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care, to prevent the unauthorized use, disclosure, dissemination, or publication of the Confidential Information as Recipient uses to protect its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall endeavor to protect the disclosed Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'using the same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care' with 'shall endeavor to protect', the standard of care is significantly weakened and becomes ambiguous. 'Endeavor' implies an attempt, but not a guaranteed or measurable level of protection, which contradicts the intent of a strict confidentiality agreement. The lack of a defined standard makes enforcement difficult.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties ag ree that they will not use or disclose (and they will cause their respective employees, agents, affiliates, representatives and consultants not to use or disclose ) in any form or for any reason any of the Confidential Information for any purpose that is not directly related to the Purpose described above.
perturbation.justification: The original text established a clear standard of care ('same degree of care, but no less than a reasonable degree of care') for protecting Confidential Information. The changed text weakens this standard to 'shall endeavor to protect', which is vague and unenforceable. This contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that the parties 'will not use or disclose' Confidential Information, thus creating a strong contradiction, as the changed text provides a much lower standard of protection.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient will return to the Disclosing Party , upon demand or in the event either Party ceases to be interested in pursuing the Purpose, all Confidential Information provided to Recipient, including all copies thereof which may have been made by or on behalf of Recipient, and Recipient shall destroy, or cause to be destroyed, all notes or memoranda or other stored information of any kind prepared by Recipient relating to the Confi dential Information or discussions generally.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will return to the Disclosing Party, upon written request from the Disclosing Party, the original Confidential Information provided to Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the requirement to return all copies and destroy notes and memoranda, limiting the obligation to only returning the original documents upon a written request. This is a contradiction of the entire agreement by not stating the distruction of notes or memorandas of any kind prepared by Recipient relating to the Confidential Information or discussions generally. This significantly weakens the protection of confidential information and contradicts the intent of ensuring complete confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the P arties desire that there be no oral or written disclosure to third par ties of the nature and extent of such discussions and information exchange regarding the Purpose , including no oral or written disclosure s to third parties of any such Confidential I nformation ; however, to the exten t disclosure is necessitated by law or ot herwise, it shall be made on the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the recipient to return all confidential information, including copies, and destroy notes/memoranda. The changed text limits this to only returning the *original* documents upon written request, omitting the destruction requirement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states there should be no disclosure to third parties and aims for complete confidentiality. By removing the destruction requirement, the modified text weakens confidentiality and creates a conflict because the recipient could retain copies and notes, potentially leading to a disclosure, conflicting with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement embodies the entire understanding between the P arties pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both P arties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement embodies the general understanding between the Parties pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the sentence requiring any additions or modifications to be made in writing and signed by both Parties. This clause is crucial for maintaining the integrity and enforceability of the contract. By removing this sentence, oral agreements or unsigned documents could potentially be argued as valid modifications, creating uncertainty and contradicting the intent of having a definitive, written agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The P arties acknowledge that, except as may be set forth in a definitive, written agreement, neither Party nor any of its directors, officers, elected or appointed officials , employees, agents, affiliates, advisors, representatives, or consultants shall have been deemed to make, or shall be responsible for, any representations or warranties, express or implied, with respect to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information supplied under this Agreement. Further, the Parties acknowledge hereby that only those representations and warranties made by the Parties in a definitive, written agreement shall have any force or effect.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause requiring all modifications to the agreement to be in writing and signed by both parties. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text states that only representations and warranties made in a definitive, written agreement shall have any force or effect. By removing the requirement for written modifications, the changed text introduces the possibility of oral or unsigned modifications being considered valid, directly contradicting the requirement in the contradicted text that only written agreements have force or effect. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, if any Recipient receives a request for Confidential Information, Recipient shall give Disclosing Party prompt written notice at the address designated herein prior to any disclosure in sufficient time to allow Disclosing Party, at its sole cost and expense, to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy concerning such disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: If any Recipient receives a request for Confidential Information, Recipient will inform Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably possible.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the 'prompt written notice' requirement to 'inform Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably possible' and removes the requirement of 'prior to any disclosure in sufficient time to allow Disclosing Party, at its sole cost and expense, to seek a protective order'. This creates a contradiction because the original clause clearly aims to provide the Disclosing Party with an opportunity to legally protect the Confidential Information before disclosure, but the modified clause is vague and does not guarantee sufficient time or opportunity for legal recourse. This weakens the protections intended by the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the P arties desire that there be no oral or written disclosure to third par ties of the nature and extent of such discussions and information exchange regarding the Purpose , including no oral or written disclosure s to third parties of any such Confidential I nformation ; however, to the exten t disclosure is necessitated by law or ot herwise, it shall be made on the terms and conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates 'prompt written notice' allowing the Disclosing Party time to seek legal recourse. The changed text weakens this to 'inform Disclosing Party as soon as reasonably possible,' removing the guarantee of sufficient time for legal action. This directly contradicts the contradicted text's aim to prevent disclosure unless 'necessitated by law or otherwise' and then only 'on the terms and conditions set forth herein'. The changed text fails to ensure those terms and conditions are met by potentially allowing disclosure before the Disclosing Party can protect its information, thus creating a clear contradiction.
